name: Keurig K-Mini Plus Single Serve K-Cup Pod Coffee Maker

specs.Width: Less than 5 inches wide — fits anywhere
specs.Tank: Single-serve fresh fill OR removable 12oz reservoir for multiple cups
specs.K-Cup: Compatible with all K-Cup pods including reusable My K-Cup
specs.Strong Mode: Strong Brew button for bolder, more concentrated taste

whatWorks: Under 5 inches wide is the defining feature of the K-Mini Plus — it fits in spaces where no other full coffee maker fits. A dorm desk, the corner of a small kitchen, a corporate cubicle, or an RV counter all have room for this. The footprint is smaller than most desktop speakers.

The removable 12-oz reservoir on the Plus model (vs. the base K-Mini which requires fresh filling each cup) lets you make 1–2 cups before refilling. It's a modest improvement over the fill-each-time design but meaningful for morning routines where you make a second cup immediately after the first.

Strong Brew mode extends the steep time to produce noticeably bolder coffee without requiring a different pod — the same K-Cup you've always bought just produces a richer result. For people who find standard K-Cup coffee weak, this is a simple fix built into the machine.

Keurig's compatibility with all K-Cup pods — including the reusable My K-Cup filter — means you're not locked into branded pods. The reusable filter opens up ground coffee from any source, which significantly lowers the per-cup cost and allows using premium ground coffee.
worthKnowing: The 12-oz removable reservoir holds only one to two cups of water. This is a compact machine designed for light, single-serve use — if you drink three or more cups daily, you'll be refilling the reservoir multiple times. The base K-Mini requires fresh filling for every single cup, which the Plus improves but doesn't eliminate.

K-Cup pod costs on the standard Keurig system run $0.50–$1.00 CAD per cup. At two cups a day for a month, that's $30–$60 in pod costs — more than drip coffee but less than Nespresso. Using the reusable My K-Cup filter with ground coffee reduces this to $0.10–$0.25 per cup.

The coffee temperature from the K-Mini Plus runs slightly lower than SCA-certified range — around 82°C–87°C vs the optimal 92°C–96°C. The coffee isn't cold, but it's noticeably lower temperature than drip machines with proper heating elements. For people who prefer slightly cooler drinking coffee, this is neutral; for those who want very hot coffee, it's a limitation.

There is no programmable timer or scheduling function — you press a button and it brews in two minutes. The auto-on/off feature simply manages idle power, not pre-brew scheduling. Unlike some full-size Keurig models, you can't tell it to have coffee ready at 7am.

commonComplaints.title: 12-oz reservoir runs out constantly…
commonComplaints.body: I make two cups every morning. I'm filling the reservoir every single day. It's a minor chore but it's every day. The tank is just too small for two-cup households.

commonComplaints.title: Coffee isn't hot enough for my taste…
commonComplaints.body: The coffee comes out warm but not hot. I want coffee I can wrap my hands around that's actually hot. This machine consistently under-heats compared to my old drip maker.

commonComplaints.title: Pod costs add up fast…
commonComplaints.body: I bought this thinking it was cheap. Then I calculated the pod math. I'm spending more on coffee per cup than before. Get the reusable filter if you care about cost.

commonComplaints.title: No way to schedule brewing…
commonComplaints.body: No timer, no scheduling. I want coffee ready when I wake up, not two minutes after I press a button. The full-size K-Supreme has this; the Mini doesn't.

verdict: The Keurig K-Mini Plus is the best compact pod coffee maker for Canadians who have very limited counter space and drink 1–2 cups daily. Under 5 inches wide, compatible with all K-Cup pods including reusable filters, and quick to brew — it does the single-serve job with minimal footprint. Ongoing pod costs, a small reservoir, and lower-than-optimal brew temperature are real limitations for heavy coffee drinkers, but for light users in tight spaces, there's no better option at the price.

realBuyers.quote: Lives on my desk at work between my monitor and a plant. If I measured the space first I wouldn't have believed it would fit, but it does.
I use the reusable My K-Cup filter with grocery store ground coffee. Works great and costs pennies per cup instead of the pod price.
Strong Brew makes a real difference. My usual pod was too weak. Strong Brew mode turned it into actually good coffee without buying anything different.
My first Keurig after years of avoiding them. The compact size is exactly what I needed. Coffee in 2 minutes, no mess, no cleanup.

sources.title: Keurig K-Mini Plus Coffee Maker — Amazon.ca
Keurig K-Mini Plus Review — Coffee Affection

metaDescription: Keurig K-Mini Plus review for Canada: under 5" wide, Strong Brew, 12-oz reservoir, all K-Cup compatible. Honest on pod costs and brew temperature. ~$119 CAD.
introText: The Keurig K-Mini Plus is Keurig's most compact single-serve coffee maker — under 5 inches wide with Strong Brew mode, a removable 12-oz reservoir, and compatibility with all K-Cup pods. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B07GV2S1GS) at around $119 CAD.
